logging(3) Library Functions Manual logging(3)

NAME

logging - Logging System


- 📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

SYNOPSIS

Files


file colorize.c
Log message colorization for terminal output. file format.c
Log format parser implementation. file json.c
📝 JSON structured logging output using yyjson file log.c
📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output file mmap.c
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging implementation. file named.c
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file colorize.h
Log message colorization for terminal output. file json.h
📝 JSON structured logging output file log.h
📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control file mmap.h
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging with crash safety. file named.h
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file types.h
Log level types and constants.

Data Structures


struct log_buffer_entry_t
A single buffered log entry.

Default log level for debug builds (DEBUG and above). #define MAX_LOG_SIZE (3 * 1024 * 1024)
Maximum log file size in bytes (3MB) before rotation. #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_SIZE (64 * 1024)
Maximum size of terminal output buffer (64KB). #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_ENTRIES 256
Maximum number of buffered log entries. #define LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 4096
Maximum size of a single log message (including formatting). #define LOG_MMAP_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 1024
Maximum size of a log message in mmap mode. #define LOG_HEADER_BUFFER_SIZE 512
Maximum size of a log header (timestamp, level, file:line:func). #define LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE 32
Maximum size of a timestamp string. #define log_plain(...)
Plain logging - writes to both log file and stderr without timestamps or log levels. #define log_plain_stderr(...)
Plain logging to stderr with newline. #define log_plain_stdout(...)
Plain logging to stdout with newline. #define log_plain_stderr_nonewline(...)
Plain logging to stderr without newline - for interactive prompts. #define log_file(...)
File-only logging - writes to log file only, no stderr output. #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)
Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)
Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). #define log_once(log_level, fmt, ...)
Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). #define LOG_CLIENT_IMPL(client, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends DEBUG log message to client. #define log_info_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends INFO log message to client. #define log_warn_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends WARN log message to client. #define log_error_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends ERROR log message to client. #define log_fatal_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends FATAL log message to client. #define LOG_SERVER_IMPL(sockfd, crypto_ctx, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends DEBUG log message to server. #define log_info_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends INFO log message to server. #define log_warn_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends WARN log message to server. #define log_error_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends ERROR log message to server. #define log_fatal_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends FATAL log message to server.

Logging Macros

Note

Compile-time log level stripping: In release builds, log_dev() and log_debug() are compiled out completely (no runtime overhead). Override with LOG_COMPILE_LEVEL.


#define log_dev(...)
Log a DEV message (most verbose, development only). #define log_debug(...)
Log a DEBUG message. #define log_info(...)
Log an INFO message. #define log_warn(...)
Log a WARN message. #define log_error(...)
Log an ERROR message. #define log_fatal(...)
Log a FATAL message.

Detailed Description

📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

This header provides a comprehensive logging system with:

  • Multiple log levels (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
  • File and terminal output with automatic color coding
  • Terminal capability detection (16-color, 256-color, truecolor)
  • Rate-limited logging macros for high-frequency logging
  • Thread-safe logging operations
  • Automatic log file rotation when size limit is reached

Note

In debug builds, log macros include file/line/function information. In release builds, this information is omitted.

This module formats named objects in log messages by scanning for hex addresses and replacing them with friendly descriptions from the named object registry when available.

For example:

  • Input: "Locked 0x7f1234567890 successfully"
  • Output: "Locked mutex: recv_mutex.2 (0x7f1234567890) successfully"

When an address is not registered, it's left unchanged.

  LOG_CLIENT_IMPL( client, level, fmt, ...)

    <include/ascii-chat/network/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((client)->crypto_initialized) {                                                                                \


    const struct crypto_context_t *_ctx = crypto_handshake_get_context(&(client)->crypto_handshake_ctx);             \


    log_net_message((client)->socket, _ctx, level, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \


                    __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                   \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)

#define
  log_every( log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \


  uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \


  uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \


  if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \


    if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \


      log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \


    }                                                                                                                \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). Logs at most once per
    specified time interval. Useful for threads that have an FPS and functions
    they call to prevent spammy logs.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


interval_us Minimum microseconds between log messages


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Each call site maintains its own static atomic timer, so
  different call sites can log independently. Thread-safe via atomic
  compare-exchange.



Uses platform_get_monotonic_time_us() for cross-platform
    time.




Definition at line 671 of file log/log.h.


671 #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)                                                                    \
672   do {                                                                                                                 \
673     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \
674     uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \
675     uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \
676     if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \
677       if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \
678         log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \
679       }                                                                                                                \
680     }                                                                                                                  \
681   } while (0)

#define
  log_nth( log_level, n, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \


  uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \


  uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \


  atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \


  if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). Logs a
    message every nth time the code is executed. Useful for logging periodic
    events in tight loops without spamming the log. For example, log_nth(INFO,
    1000, "Processed items") logs every 1000 calls.




Each call site maintains its own static counter, so different call
    sites can log independently at different frequencies.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


n Log every nth call (1 = every call, 2 = every 2nd call, etc.)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic fetch_add.

739 #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)                                                                                \
740   do {                                                                                                                 \
741     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \
742     uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \
743     uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \
744     atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \
745     if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \
746       log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \
747     }                                                                                                                  \
748   } while (0)










#define
  log_once( log_level, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_once_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                            \


  uint64_t _log_once_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_once_counter);                         \


  if (_log_once_count == 0) {                                                                                        \


    atomic_store_u64(&_log_once_counter, 1);                                              \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). Logs a message
    exactly once, no matter how many times the code is executed. Each call site
    maintains its own static counter, so different call sites can log
    independently.




Useful for one-time initialization messages, warnings, or debug
    output that should only appear once per session.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic operations.

enum log_color_t


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Color enum for logging - indexes into color arrays. These values
    directly index into level_colors arrays. Order matches DEV, DEBUG, WARN,
    INFO, ERROR, FATAL, GREY, RESET.


Enumerator
  





  
LOG_COLOR_DEV
    

  
Blue - DEV messages

  
LOG_COLOR_DEBUG
    

  
Cyan - DEBUG messages

  
LOG_COLOR_INFO
    

  
Green - INFO messages

  
LOG_COLOR_WARN
    

  
Yellow - WARN messages

  
LOG_COLOR_ERROR
    

  
Red - ERROR messages

  
LOG_COLOR_FATAL
    

  
Magenta - FATAL messages

  
LOG_COLOR_GREY
    

  
Grey - for neutral messages or labels

  
LOG_COLOR_RESET
    

  
Reset to default

enum log_level_t


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/types.h>


Logging levels enumeration. Defines the severity levels for log
    messages. Used throughout the logging system without circular
  dependencies.




Note: If raylib is included (detected via RAYLIB_H), we skip
    redefining the enumerators and just use int for log_level_t, since
    raylib already defines LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_ERROR, LOG_FATAL with
    compatible values.


Enumerator
  





  
LOG_DEV
    

  
Development messages (most verbose)

  
LOG_DEBUG
    

  
Debug messages

  
LOG_INFO
    

  
Informational messages

  
LOG_WARN
    

  
Warning messages

  
LOG_ERROR
    

  
Error messages

  
LOG_FATAL
    

  
Fatal error messages (most severe)

Function
  Documentation




const
  char * colorize_named_string (const char * name_str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Colorize a "type/name" string for display.


Parameters


name_str String in "type/name" or
  "type/name.id" format


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes:
  type(yellow)/name(blue)


Used by memory report and other displays to colorize named
    objects. Applies colors:


    
  
  • Type: yellow (LOG_COLOR_WARN)
    • 
  
  • Name: blue (LOG_COLOR_DEV)
    • 
  
  • Separator: uncolored
    • 



Example: "thread/splash_anim.0" displays with type in
    yellow, name in blue




Note


Returns pointer to rotating static buffer (4 buffers)



Safe to use in printf-style calls: "%s"




Colorize a "type/name" string for display.




Parameters


name_str String in "type/name
  (0xaddress)" format (e.g., "thread/splash_anim.0
  (0x7f123456)")


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes: type(yellow)/name(blue)
  (id(grey))


Used by memory report to colorize named object display. Returns a
    pointer to a static rotating buffer (4 buffers, valid until next call).




Example: "thread/splash_anim.0 (0x7f123456)" displays
    with:


    
  
  • "thread" in yellow
    • 
  
  • "splash_anim.0" in blue
    • 
  
  • "0x7f123456" in grey
    • 



Definition at line 976 of file log/named.c.


976                                                         {
977 #define COLORIZE_BUFFERS 4
978 #define COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE 512
979   static char buffers[COLORIZE_BUFFERS][COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE];
980   static int buffer_idx = 0;
981 
982   if (!name_str || name_str[0] == '\0') {
983     return name_str;
984   }
985 
986   char *current_buf = buffers[buffer_idx];
987   buffer_idx = (buffer_idx + 1) % COLORIZE_BUFFERS;
988 
989   /* Find the slash separator (type/name) */
990   const char *slash = strchr(name_str, '/');
991   if (!slash) {
992     /* No slash found - just return the string as-is */
993     return name_str;
994   }
995 
996   /* Find opening paren for address */
997   const char *paren = strchr(name_str, '(');
998 
999   /* Extract type (before slash) */
1000   size_t type_len = slash - name_str;
1001   char type_buf[256];
1002   strncpy(type_buf, name_str, type_len < sizeof(type_buf) - 1 ? type_len : sizeof(type_buf) - 1);
1003   type_buf[type_len] = '\0';
1004 
1005   /* Extract name (between slash and paren, or end of string) */
1006   const char *name_start = slash + 1;
1007   size_t name_len;
1008   if (paren && paren > slash) {
1009     /* Skip spaces before paren */
1010     const char *name_end = paren - 1;
1011     while (name_end > name_start && *name_end == ' ') {
1012       name_end--;
1013     }
1014     name_len = name_end - name_start + 1;
1015   } else {
1016     name_len = strlen(name_start);
1017   }
1018 
1019   char name_buf[256];
1020   strncpy(name_buf, name_start, name_len < sizeof(name_buf) - 1 ? name_len : sizeof(name_buf) - 1);
1021   name_buf[name_len] = '\0';
1022 
1023   /* Apply colors */
1024   const char *type_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, type_buf);
1025   const char *name_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, name_buf);
1026 
1027   /* Format: type(yellow)/name(blue) and add address part if present */
1028   if (paren) {
1029     /* Extract address (0x...) from parentheses */
1030     const char *addr_start = paren + 1;
1031     const char *addr_end = strchr(addr_start, ')');
1032     if (addr_end) {
1033       size_t addr_len = addr_end - addr_start;
1034       char addr_buf[128];
1035       strncpy(addr_buf, addr_start, addr_len < sizeof(addr_buf) - 1 ? addr_len : sizeof(addr_buf) - 1);
1036       addr_buf[addr_len] = '\0';
1037 
1038       const char *addr_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, addr_buf);
1039       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s (%s)", type_colored, name_colored, addr_colored);
1040     } else {
1041       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s %s", type_colored, name_colored, paren);
1042     }
1043   } else {
1044     safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s", type_colored, name_colored);
1045   }
1046 
1047   return current_buf;
1048 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFERS
1049 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE
1050 }






References colored_string(), COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE,
    COLORIZE_BUFFERS, LOG_COLOR_DEV, LOG_COLOR_GREY,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, and safe_snprintf().

  * format_message (const char * format, va_list args)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Format a message using va_list.


Parameters


format Format string


args Variable arguments list


Returns


Formatted message string (must be freed by caller)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get current time as formatted string.


Parameters


time_buf Output buffer for formatted time


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null
  terminator)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Destroy the logging system and close log file.


Definition at line 623 of file log/log.c.


623                        {
624   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
625   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
626     log_mmap_destroy();
627   }
628 
629   // Cleanup grep filter
630   grep_destroy();
631 
632   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
633    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
634    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
635    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
636   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
637 
638   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
639   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
640   if (g_log.format) {
641     log_template_free(g_log.format);
642     g_log.format = NULL;
643   }
644   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
645     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
646     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
647   }
648   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
649 
650   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
651   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
652   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
653     platform_close(old_file);
654   }
655   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
656 
657   // Destroy rotation mutex
658   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
659 }






References atomic_load_int(), atomic_store_bool(),

asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap (const char *
  log_path)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap-based logging. When enabled, log messages
    bypass the mutex and use atomic operations to write directly to a
    memory-mapped log file as human-readable text.




Benefits:


    
  
  • No mutex contention between logging threads
    • 
  
  • Crash-safe: text is written directly to mmap'd file, readable after
    crash
    • 
  
  • Fast path uses atomic fetch_add, no locks
    • 
  
  • ERROR/FATAL messages sync immediately for visibility
    • 
  
  • Simple: log file IS the mmap file (no separate binary format)
    • 



Parameters


log_path Path to the log file (will be
  memory-mapped)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Call log_init() first, then
  log_enable_mmap() to upgrade to lock-free


Definition at line 1752 of file log/log.c.


1752                                                         {
1753   return log_enable_mmap_sized(log_path, 0); /* Use default size */
1754 }






References log_enable_mmap_sized().






asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap_sized (const char *
  log_path, size_t max_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap logging with custom file size.

Get the current log format template (opaque pointer).

    same template as the rest of the logging system (e.g., platform code).

  the logging system

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Initialize the logging system.


Parameters


filename Log file path (or NULL for no file
  logging)


level Minimum log level to output


force_stderr If true, route ALL logs to stderr (for client mode to keep
  stdout clean)


use_mmap If true, use fully lock-free mmap logging (recommended). If mmap
  fails, uses stderr only (no mutex fallback).


Note


When use_mmap=true, the entire logging path is lock-free:

    
  
  • File output uses atomic operations on mmap'd memory
    • 
  
  • Terminal output uses atomic fprintf/fwrite to fd
    • 
  
  • No mutex is ever acquired in the hot path
    • 





Definition at line 533 of file log/log.c.


533                                                                                          {
534   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
535   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
536   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
537   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
538 
539   // Set basic config using atomic stores
540   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
541   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
542 
543   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
544   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
545   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
546   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
547     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
548   }
549 
550   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
551   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
552   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
553     platform_close(old_file);
554     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
555   }
556 
557   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
558   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
559   if (env_level_str) {
560     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
561   } else {
562     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
563   }
564 
565   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
566   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
567 
568   if (filename) {
569     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
570 
571     if (use_mmap) {
572       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
573       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
574       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
575         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
576       } else {
577         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
578         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
579           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
580         }
581         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
582         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
583       }
584     } else {
585       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
586       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
587       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
588       if (fd < 0) {
589         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
590           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
591         }
592         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
593       }
594     }
595   } else {
596     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
597     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
598   }
599 
600   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
601   log_set_format(NULL, false);
602 
603   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
604   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
605     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
606   }
607 
608   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
609 
610   // Reset terminal detection if needed
611   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
612     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
613   }
614 
615   // Detect terminal capabilities
616   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
617 
618   // NOTE: Color initialization happens separately via log_set_color_scheme()
619   // after options are parsed. Logging works without colors until then.
620   // NOTE: Grep filter initialization happens in main.c after options_init() completes.
621 }

Initialize logging color system with current terminal

1661   /* Skip color initialization during terminal detection to avoid mutex deadlock */
1662   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1663     return;
1664   }
1665 
1666   /* Skip color initialization before logging is fully initialized */
1667   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
1668     return;
1669   }
1670 
1671   if (g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1672     return;
1673   }
1674 
1675   /* Get active color scheme - this ensures color system is initialized */
1676   const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
1677   if (!scheme) {
1678     /* Don't mark as initialized if we can't get a color scheme - return NULL instead */
1679     return;
1680   }
1681 
1682   /* Acquire mutex for compilation (mutex is now initialized by colorscheme_init) */
1683   mutex_lock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1684   /* Debug: Check if g_log.compiled_colors is actually zero-initialized */
1685   /* Zero the structure on first use to avoid freeing garbage pointers */
1686   /* (static = {0} produces garbage in this build for unknown reasons) */

1690     first_compile = false;
1691   }
1692 
1693   /* Detect terminal background */
1694   terminal_background_t background = detect_terminal_background();
1695   /* Determine color mode for compilation */
1696   terminal_color_mode_t mode;
1697   if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
1698     mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
1699   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1700     mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
1701   } else {
1702     mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
1703   }
1704   /* Compile the color scheme to ANSI codes */
1705   asciichat_error_t result = colorscheme_compile_scheme(scheme, mode, background, &g_log.compiled_colors);
1706   g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized = true;
1707   mutex_unlock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1708 
1709   /* Log outside of mutex lock to avoid recursive lock deadlock */
1710   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1711     log_debug("Failed to compile color scheme: %d", result);
1712   }
1713 }

bool log_lock_terminal (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Lock terminal output for exclusive access by the calling thread.
    Call this before interactive prompts (like password entry, yes/no questions)
    to ensure only the calling thread can output to the terminal. Other threads'
    log messages will be buffered and flushed when the terminal is unlocked.




While locked:


    
  
  • The locking thread can use log_plain() to write to terminal
    • 
  
  • Other threads' log messages go to log file and are buffered
    • 
  
  • Buffered messages are flushed to terminal on unlock
    • 



Must be paired with log_unlock_terminal().




Returns


The previous terminal lock state (for nested calls)

787   while (!atomic_cas_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked, &expected, true)) {
788     // Retry if CAS failed
789   }
790   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread, (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self());
791   return expected;
792 }

1047

1056       uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1057       get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);
1058 
1059       // Choose output stream using unified routing logic
1060       int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1061       FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1062       // Check if colors should be used
1063       // Priority 1: If --color was explicitly passed, force colors
1064       bool use_colors = true; // Default: enable colors
1065       if (g_color_flag_passed && !g_color_flag_value) {
1066         use_colors = false; // --color=false explicitly disables colors
1067       }
1068       // Priority 2: If --color NOT explicitly passed, enable colors by default
1069

1071       int header_len = format_log_header(header_buffer, sizeof(header_buffer), level, time_buf, file, line, func,
1072                                          use_colors, time_ns);
1073 
1074       if (header_len >= 0 && header_len < (int)sizeof(header_buffer)) {

1076           const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(msg_buffer);
1077           const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
1078           if (colors) {
1079             safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s%s%s\n", header_buffer, colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET], colorized_msg,
1080                          colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET]);
1081           } else {
1082             safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s\n", header_buffer, colorized_msg);
1083           }
1084         } else {
1085           safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s%s\n", header_buffer, msg_buffer);
1086         }
1087         (void)fflush(output_stream);
1088       }
1089     }
1090     return;
1091   }
1092   /* =========================================================================
1093    * FILE I/O PATH: Lock-free using atomic write() syscalls
1094    * ========================================================================= */
1095   char time_buf[LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE];
1096   uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1097   get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);
1098   // Format message for file output
1099   char log_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1100   va_list args;
1101   va_start(args, fmt);
1102   int header_len = format_log_header(log_buffer, sizeof(log_buffer), level, time_buf, file, line, func, false, time_ns);
1103   if (header_len < 0 || header_len >= (int)sizeof(log_buffer)) {
1104     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format log header");
1105     va_end(args);
1106     return;
1107   }
1108   int msg_len = header_len;
1109   int formatted_len = safe_vsnprintf(log_buffer + header_len, sizeof(log_buffer) - (size_t)header_len, fmt, args);
1110   if (formatted_len < 0) {

1113     return;
1114   }

1117   // Truncate at whole line boundaries to avoid UTF-8 issues

1119   // Add newline if there's room and message doesn't already end with one
1120   if (msg_len > 0 && msg_len < (int)sizeof(log_buffer) - 1) {
1121     if (log_buffer[msg_len - 1] != '\n') {
1122       log_buffer[msg_len++] = '\n';
1123       log_buffer[msg_len] = '\0';

1125   }

1129 
1130   // Extract the user message part (without header) for JSON logging
1131   const char *user_message = log_buffer + header_len;
1132 
1133   // For JSON output: strip automatic trailing newline (keep explicit ones)
1134   char json_message_buf[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1135   const char *json_message = user_message;
1136   size_t user_msg_len = strlen(user_message);
1137 
1138   // If message ends with newline and it was added automatically (not in original message)
1139   if (user_msg_len > 0 && user_message[user_msg_len - 1] == '\n') {
1140     // The newline was added automatically if the message part didn't end with one
1141     // before we added it on line 1067. We can check this by looking at msg_len - 1
1142     // If msg_len >= header_len + 2, then msg_len - 2 would be the char before the newline
1143     int msg_content_len = msg_len - header_len;
1144     // NOLINTNEXTLINE(clang-analyzer-security.ArrayBound) - msg_content_len > 1 bounds check above
1145     if (msg_content_len > 1 && log_buffer[msg_len - 2] != '\n') {
1146       // Newline was added automatically - strip it for JSON
1147       if (user_msg_len - 1 < sizeof(json_message_buf)) {
1148         memcpy(json_message_buf, user_message, user_msg_len - 1);
1149         json_message_buf[user_msg_len - 1] = '\0';
1150         json_message = json_message_buf;
1151       }
1152     }
1153   }
1154 
1155   // Check if JSON format is enabled
1156   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
1157   bool json_format_enabled = (json_fd >= 0);
1158 
1159   // If JSON format is enabled, output ONLY JSON (skip text output)
1160   if (json_format_enabled) {
1161     // Output JSON to the JSON file descriptor
1162     log_json_write(json_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1163     // Also output JSON to console using unified routing logic, respecting quiet flag
1164     if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1165       int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1166       log_json_write(console_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1167     }
1168   } else {
1169     // Text format: output to file and terminal
1170     // No heap allocation - use log_buffer directly
1171     // Write to file (atomic write syscall) - use original buffer (with ANSI codes)
1172     int file_fd = (int)atomic_load_u64(&g_log.file);
1173     if (file_fd >= 0 && file_fd != STDERR_FILENO) {
1174       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1175     }
1176 
1177     // Write to terminal (atomic state checks)
1178     va_list args_terminal;
1179     va_start(args_terminal, fmt);
1180     write_to_terminal_atomic(level, time_buf, file, line, func, fmt, args_terminal, time_ns);
1181     va_end(args_terminal);
1182   }
1183 }

int
  log_named_format_message (const char * message, char * output, size_t
  output_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format hex addresses in a message as named object
  descriptions.


Parameters


message Input message (may contain hex addresses
  like 0x123456)


output Output buffer for formatted message


output_size Size of output buffer (must be > 0)


Returns


Length of output string (excluding null terminator) if
  any formatting was applied, -1 if no formatting was applied or on error


Scans the input message for hex addresses in the format
    0x[0-9a-fA-F]+. For each address found, checks the named object registry. If
    registered, replaces the address with "type: name (0xaddress)"
    format. If not registered, leaves the original hex address unchanged.




The output buffer is always null-terminated.




Note


The formatting is idempotent - applying it twice gives
  the same result



This function is efficient for messages with few hex addresses
    (common case)




Works correctly with 32-bit and 64-bit address formats




Definition at line 595 of file log/named.c.


595                                                                                     {
596   if (!message || !output || output_size == 0) {
597     return -1;
598   }
599 
600   size_t out_pos = 0;
601   const char *p = message;
602   bool any_transformed = false;
603 
604   while (*p && out_pos < output_size - 1) {
605     /* Look for "0x" prefix indicating a hex address */
606     if (*p == '0' && *(p + 1) == 'x' && isxdigit(*(p + 2))) {
607       const char *hex_start = p;
608       if (try_format_hex_address(message, hex_start, output, output_size, &out_pos, &p)) {
609         any_transformed = true;
610         continue;
611       }
612 
613       /* Not registered or formatting failed - copy original hex address */
614       const char *hex_end = hex_start + 2; /* Start after "0x" */
615       while (*hex_end && isxdigit(*hex_end)) {
616         hex_end++;
617       }
618       size_t hex_len = hex_end - hex_start;
619       if (hex_len < output_size - out_pos) {
620         memcpy(output + out_pos, hex_start, hex_len);
621         out_pos += hex_len;
622         p = hex_end;
623       } else {
624         /* Buffer overflow - truncate and stop */
625         break;
626       }
627     } else if (isdigit(*p)) {
628       /* Look for decimal integers that might be FDs or packet types */
629       const char *int_start = p;
630       int fd_value = 0;
631       int digit_count = 0;
632 
633       /* Parse decimal digits */
634       while (*p && isdigit(*p) && digit_count < 6) { /* FDs rarely exceed 999999 */
635         fd_value = fd_value * 10 + (*p - '0');
636         p++;
637         digit_count++;
638       }
639 
640       /* Skip if this integer is part of already-formatted output like "(fd=20)" or "(type/name (...))" */
641       bool is_already_formatted = false;
642       if (int_start - message >= 5) {
643         /* Check for "(fd=", "(pkt_type=", "(socket=", "(sockfd=", or "(type/" patterns to prevent re-formatting.
644          * Named objects are formatted as: type/name (0xaddress) or type/name (key=value)
645          * We need to detect if we're already inside a formatted output.
646          */
647         const char *check = int_start - 1;
648         while (check > message && isspace(*check))
649           check--;
650 
651         // Only access *check if pointer is within bounds
652         if (check >= message && *check == '=') {
653           /* Found "=", now check what prefix it has for patterns like "(fd=20)" or "(socket=20)" or "(pkt_type=123)" */
654           const char *eq_pos = check;
655           check--;
656           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
657             check--;
658           }
659           check++;
660 
661           size_t prefix_len = eq_pos - check;
662           if ((prefix_len == 2 && strncmp(check, "fd", 2) == 0) ||
663               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "socket", 6) == 0) ||
664               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "sockfd", 6) == 0) ||
665               (prefix_len == 8 && strncmp(check, "pkt_type", 8) == 0)) {
666             /* Only mark as already-formatted if inside formatted output:
667              * - "(" before keyword: parenthesized value like "(sockfd=15)"
668              * - "/" before keyword: name part of type/name like "fd/fd=7"
669              * Raw log patterns like "sockfd=15" should still be eligible for replacement. */
670             const char *before_kw = check - 1;
671             while (before_kw > message && isspace(*before_kw))
672               before_kw--;
673             if (before_kw >= message && (*before_kw == '(' || *before_kw == '/')) {
674               is_already_formatted = true;
675             }
676           }
677         } else if (check >= message && *check == '/') {
678           /* Found "/", now check if this is a type/name pattern.
679            * Format is "(type/name (...)" where type is word characters (thread, mutex, socket, etc.)
680            * Must verify "/" is preceded by word characters AND "(" to ensure it's a formatted type.
681            */
682           const char *slash_pos = check;
683           check--;
684           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
685             check--;
686           }
687           check++;
688 
689           /* Check that the "/" was preceded by word characters (indicating a type name) */
690           size_t type_len = slash_pos - check;
691           if (type_len > 0 && (isalpha(*check) || *check == '_')) {
692             /* Verify this is a registered type/name pattern by checking for "(" before the type
693              * AND checking if the type actually exists in the registry */
694             const char *type_start = check;
695             const char *before_type = type_start - 1;
696             while (before_type > message && isspace(*before_type)) {
697               before_type--;
698             }
699 
700             /* Only mark as formatted if:
701              * 1. Preceded by "(" - ensures pattern looks like "(type/name ...)"
702              * 2. Type exists in registry - prevents false positives on random text */
703             if (before_type >= message && *before_type == '(' && is_type_in_registry(type_start, type_len)) {
704               is_already_formatted = true;
705             }
706           }
707         }
708       }
709 
710       /* Check if this is a registered packet type */
711       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_packet_type_prefix(message, int_start)) {
712         const char *name = named_get_packet_type(fd_value);
713         if (name) {
714           const char *prefix_start = find_packet_type_prefix_start(message, int_start);
715           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
716           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
717 
718           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
719             out_pos -= prefix_len;
720           }
721 
722           if (write_formatted_packet_type(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
723             any_transformed = true;
724             continue;
725           }
726 
727           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
728         }
729       }
730 
731 
732       /* Check if this is a registered file descriptor */
733       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_fd_prefix(message, int_start)) {
734         const char *name = named_get_fd(fd_value);
735         const char *reg_type = NULL;
736 
737         /* Fallback: check main registry for sockets registered via NAMED_REGISTER_SOCKET
738          * (which uses raw integer key, not FD namespace encoding) */
739         if (!name) {
740           name = named_get((uintptr_t)(intptr_t)fd_value);
741           if (name) {
742             reg_type = named_get_type((uintptr_t)(intptr_t)fd_value);
743           }
744         }
745 
746         if (name) {
747           const char *prefix_start = find_fd_prefix_start(message, int_start);
748           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
749           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
750 
751           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
752             out_pos -= prefix_len;
753           }
754 
755           bool formatted = false;
756           if (reg_type) {
757             /* From main registry — use registered type and original prefix keyword */
758             const char *kw_end = prefix_start;
759             while (kw_end < int_start && (isalnum(*kw_end) || *kw_end == '_'))
760               kw_end++;
761             size_t kw_len = kw_end - prefix_start;
762 
763             char kw_buf[32];
764             if (kw_len > 0 && kw_len < sizeof(kw_buf)) {
765               memcpy(kw_buf, prefix_start, kw_len);
766               kw_buf[kw_len] = '\0';
767             } else {
768               snprintf(kw_buf, sizeof(kw_buf), "%s", reg_type);
769             }
770 
771             char temp[512];
772             int written = snprintf(temp, sizeof(temp), "%s/%s (%s=%d)", reg_type, name, kw_buf, fd_value);
773             if (written > 0 && (size_t)written < sizeof(temp) && out_pos + (size_t)written < output_size - 1) {
774               memcpy(output + out_pos, temp, (size_t)written);
775               out_pos += (size_t)written;
776               formatted = true;
777             }
778           } else {
779             formatted = write_formatted_fd(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos);
780           }
781 
782           if (formatted) {
783             any_transformed = true;
784             continue;
785           }
786 
787           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
788         }
789       }
790 
791       /* Check if this integer has a generic type prefix (socket, client, connection, etc.)
792        * DISABLED: This feature has a backtracking bug that corrupts output when applied after
793        * other formatting operations. Re-enable only after fixing the backtracking logic to
794        * properly track correspondence between input and output positions.
795        * See commit b86fed2a8 which introduced the bug.
796        */
797       // NOLINTNEXTLINE(readability-simplify-boolean-expr) - Intentionally disabled code, see comment above
798       if (false && !is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0) {
799         const char *type_name = NULL;
800         size_t type_len = 0;
801         const char *prefix_start = find_generic_type_prefix(message, int_start, &type_name, &type_len);
802 
803         if (type_name && prefix_start != int_start) {
804           /* Skip if already formatted with a name (e.g., "socket/listener.0" already has a /) */
805           bool skip_format = false;
806           /* Check if this looks like it's already been formatted by looking for / in the pattern */
807           const char *check_ptr = prefix_start;
808           while (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr && *check_ptr != '/') {
809             check_ptr++;
810           }
811           if (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr == '/') {
812             /* Already formatted (contains /), skip */
813             skip_format = true;
814           }
815 
816           if (!skip_format) {
817             /* We found a type prefix like "socket", "client", etc.
818              * Try to look up a registered name for this type+id */
819 
820             /* Normalize type name aliases: "sockfd" → "socket", "descriptor" → "fd" */
821             const char *lookup_type = type_name;
822             size_t lookup_len = type_len;
823             if (type_len == 6 && strncmp(type_name, "sockfd", 6) == 0) {
824               lookup_type = "socket";
825               lookup_len = 6;
826             } else if (type_len == 10 && strncmp(type_name, "descriptor", 10) == 0) {
827               lookup_type = "fd";
828               lookup_len = 2;
829             }
830 
831             const char *name = named_get_by_type_and_id(lookup_type, lookup_len, fd_value);
832 
833             char temp_output[512];
834             char type_str[32];
835             char id_buffer[64];
836 
837             /* Safely copy type name */
838             if (type_len < sizeof(type_str)) {
839               memcpy(type_str, type_name, type_len);
840               type_str[type_len] = '\0';
841 
842               int id_written = snprintf(id_buffer, sizeof(id_buffer), "%d", fd_value);
843               if (id_written > 0 && id_written < (int)sizeof(id_buffer)) {
844                 int temp_written = 0;
845                 if (name) {
846                   /* Format with name: type/name (type=value) */
847                   temp_written =
848                       snprintf(temp_output, sizeof(temp_output), "%s/%s (%s=%s)", type_str, name, type_str, id_buffer);
849 
850                   if (temp_written > 0 && (size_t)temp_written < sizeof(temp_output)) {
851                     /* Backtrack to remove the prefix we already copied */
852                     size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
853                     if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
854                       out_pos -= prefix_len;
855                     }
856 
857                     int copy_len = temp_written;
858                     if (out_pos + copy_len < output_size - 1) {
859                       memcpy(output + out_pos, temp_output, copy_len);
860                       out_pos += copy_len;
861                       any_transformed = true;
862                       continue;
863                     }
864                   }
865                 }
866               }
867             }
868           }
869         }
870       }
871 
872       /* Not a registered FD or formatting failed - copy original number */
873       if (!copy_unformatted_decimal(int_start, p, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
874         break;
875       }
876     } else {
877       /* Check if this is the start of "type=DIGIT" pattern */
878       if (*p == 't' && p + 4 < message + strlen(message) && is_type_equals_pattern(p)) {
879         if (!type_equals_inside_parens(p, message)) {
880           const char *digit_end = p + 5;
881           int pkt_value = parse_decimal_digits(digit_end, &digit_end);
882 
883           if (pkt_value > 0) {
884             const char *name = named_get_packet_type(pkt_value);
885             if (name && write_formatted_type_equals(pkt_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
886               p = digit_end;
887               any_transformed = true;

890           }
891         }
892       }
893 
894       /* Regular character - copy it */

896     }
897   }
898 
899   /* Null terminate */
900   if (out_pos < output_size) {
901     output[out_pos] = '\0';
902   } else {
903     output[output_size - 1] = '\0';
904   }
905 
906   return any_transformed ? (int)out_pos : -1;
907 }

Referenced by log_named_format_or_original().






const
  char * log_named_format_or_original (const char * message)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format named objects in a message (thread-local buffer).


Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string if any formatting was applied,
  original message if no formatting was applied


Convenience wrapper that handles buffer management internally
    using a thread-local buffer. The returned pointer is valid only until the
    next call to this function from the same thread.




Safe to use directly in log macros:




log_info("State: %s", log_named_format_or_original(msg));



Note


Maximum formatted message length is 4096 bytes



If format buffer overflows, returns original message




Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string (thread-local buffer, valid only
  until next call)


Convenience wrapper that uses a thread-local buffer internally.
    Applies formatting iteratively until the message stops changing (fixpoint).
    Returns the original message if no formatting was applied.


Definition at line 918 of file log/named.c.


918                                                               {
919   if (!message) {
920     return message;
921   }
922 
923   static _Thread_local char format_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
924   static _Thread_local char work_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
925 
926   /* Apply formatting iteratively until the message stabilizes (no more changes) */
927   const char *current = message;
928   size_t max_iterations = 3; /* Reduce iterations to prevent recursion (prevent transforming already-transformed output) */
929 
930   for (size_t iter = 0; iter < max_iterations; iter++) {
931     int result = log_named_format_message(current, format_buffer, sizeof(format_buffer));
932 
933     if (result <= 0) {
934       /* No transformation was made, we've reached a fixpoint */
935       return current == message ? message : format_buffer;
936     }
937 
938     /* Check if the message changed */
939     if (strcmp(current, format_buffer) == 0) {
940       /* No actual change, return the current version */
941       return format_buffer;
942     }
943 
944     /* Message changed, prepare for next iteration */
945     if (current == message) {
946       /* First iteration, use format_buffer as input for next iteration */
947       current = format_buffer;
948     } else {
949       /* Subsequent iterations, swap buffers */
950       asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer), format_buffer);
951       if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
952         log_error("Failed to copy format buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
953         return format_buffer;
954       }
955       current = work_buffer;
956     }
957   }
958 
959   /* Max iterations reached, return last formatted version */
960   return format_buffer;
961 }

    log_named_format_message(), NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE, and
    SAFE_STRCPY.

1246 
1247       if (header_len > 0) {
1248         write_to_log_file_atomic(header_buffer, header_len, NULL);
1249       }
1250       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1251       write_to_log_file_atomic("\n", 1, NULL);
1252     }
1253   }
1254 
1255   // Terminal output (atomic state checks)
1256   if (!atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1257     return;
1258   }
1259   if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_locked)) {
1260     uint64_t owner = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread);
1261     if (owner != (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self()) {
1262       return;
1263     }
1264   }
1265 
1266   // Check if JSON format is enabled
1267   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
1268   bool json_format_enabled = (json_fd >= 0);
1269 
1270   if (json_format_enabled) {
1271     // Output JSON for plain messages too
1272     uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1273     int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1274     log_json_write(console_fd, LOG_INFO, time_ns, __FILE__, __LINE__, "log_plain_msg", log_buffer);
1275   } else {
1276     // Choose output stream using unified routing logic (LOG_INFO level)
1277     int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1278     FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1279 
1280     // Apply colorization for TTY output
1281     if (terminal_should_color_output(fd)) {
1282       const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(log_buffer);
1283       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", colorized_msg);
1284     } else {
1285       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", log_buffer);
1286     }
1287     (void)fflush(output_stream);
1288   }
1289 }






References args, asciichat_thread_self(),
    atomic_load_int(), atomic_load_u64(),
    colorize_log_message(), get_current_time_formatted(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), LOG_INFO, log_json_write(),
    log_mmap_is_active(), log_mmap_write(),
    LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE, LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE,
    safe_fprintf(), safe_vsnprintf(),
    shutdown_is_requested(), terminal_choose_log_fd(),
    terminal_should_color_output(), and
  time_get_realtime_ns().

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Converts a plain text log line (from log file) into a colored version
    matching the format used for terminal output. Applies colors to:


    
  
  • Timestamp and level based on log level
    • 
  
  • Thread ID in grey
    • 
  
  • File path in cyan
    • 
  
  • Line number in magenta
    • 
  
  • Function name in orange/DEV color
    • 
  
  • Message body colorized appropriately
    • 



Expected plain format (debug mode): [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL]
    [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE




Parameters


plain_line Plain text log line (from log file)


colored_buf Output buffer for colored version (must be large enough)


buf_size Size of colored_buf


Returns


Length of colored string, 0 on error or invalid
  format


Note


Uses static buffer internally, result is reused across
  calls


Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID]
    FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes
    matching the colored format.


Definition at line 1858 of file log/log.c.


1858                                                                                            {
1859   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1860     return 0;
1861   }
1862 
1863   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1864 
1865   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1866   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1867   const char *p = plain_line;
1868 
1869   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1870   if (*p != '[') {
1871     return 0; // Invalid format
1872   }
1873   p++;
1874   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1875 
1876   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1877   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1878   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1879     p++;
1880   }
1881   if (*p != ']') {
1882     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1883   }
1884 
1885   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1886   char timestamp[64];
1887   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1888     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1889   }
1890   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1891   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1892   p++; // Skip ]
1893 
1894   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1895   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1896     p++;
1897   }
1898 
1899   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1900   if (*p != '[') {
1901     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1902   }
1903   p++;
1904   const char *level_start = p;
1905   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1906     p++;
1907   }
1908   if (*p != ']') {
1909     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1910   }
1911   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1912   char level_str[16];
1913   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1914     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1915   }
1916   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1917   strncpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1918   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1919 
1920   // Determine log level for color selection
1921   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1922   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1923     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1924   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1925     level = LOG_INFO;
1926   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1927     level = LOG_WARN;
1928   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1929     level = LOG_ERROR;
1930   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1931     level = LOG_FATAL;
1932   }
1933 
1934   p++; // Skip ]
1935 
1936   // Skip whitespace after level
1937   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1938     p++;
1939   }
1940 
1941   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1942   uint64_t tid = 0;
1943   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1944     p += 5;
1945     char *tid_end = NULL;
1946     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1947     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1948       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1949       // Try to find the closing bracket
1950       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1951         p++;
1952       }
1953       if (*p == ']') {
1954         p++;
1955       }
1956     } else {
1957       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1958     }
1959     // Skip whitespace after tid
1960     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1961       p++;
1962     }
1963   }
1964   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1965 
1966   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1967   const char *file_start = p;
1968   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1969     p++;
1970   }
1971   if (*p != ':') {
1972     return 0; // Malformed
1973   }
1974   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1975   char file_path[256];
1976   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1977     return 0;
1978   }
1979   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1980   strncpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
1981   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
1982   p++; // Skip :
1983 
1984   // Extract line number (digits only)
1985   int line_num = 0;
1986   const char *line_start = p;
1987   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
1988     p++;
1989   }
1990   if (p == line_start) {
1991     return 0; // No line number
1992   }
1993   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
1994 
1995   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
1996   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
1997     p++;
1998   }
1999 
2000   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
2001   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
2002     p += 3; // Skip "in "
2003   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
2004     // Allow tab after "in"
2005     p += 2; // Skip "in"
2006     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
2007       p++;
2008     }
2009   } else {
2010     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
2011     return 0;
2012   }
2013 
2014   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
2015   const char *func_start = p;
2016   while (*p && *p != '(') {
2017     p++;
2018   }
2019   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
2020     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
2021   }
2022   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
2023 
2024   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
2025   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
2026     func_len--;
2027   }
2028 
2029   char func_name[256];
2030   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
2031     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
2032   }
2033   if (func_len > 0) {
2034     /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
2035     strncpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
2036   }
2037   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
2038 
2039   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
2040   if (*p == '(') {
2041     p++;
2042     while (*p && *p != ')') {
2043       p++;
2044     }
2045     if (*p == ')') {
2046       p++;
2047     }
2048   }
2049 
2050   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
2051   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
2052     p++;
2053   }
2054 
2055   // Remaining is the message
2056   const char *message = p;
2057 
2058   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
2059   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
2060   if (!colors) {
2061     // No colors available, return plain text
2062     static bool warned_once = false;
2063     if (!warned_once) {
2064       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
2065       warned_once = true;
2066     }
2067     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2068     if (len >= buf_size) {
2069       return 0;
2070     }
2071     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2072     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2073     return len;
2074   }
2075 
2076   // Build colored output
2077   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2078   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2079   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2080   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2081   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2082   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2083 
2084   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2085                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2086                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2087                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2088 
2089   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2090     return 0;
2091   }
2092 
2093   // Colorize the message part
2094   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2095   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2096                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2097                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2098                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2099 
2100   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2101     return 0;
2102   }
2103 
2104   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2105   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2106   return (size_t)len;
2107 }

void
  log_redetect_terminal_capabilities (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Re-detect terminal capabilities after logging is initialized.
    Useful when terminal capabilities change or need to be refreshed.


Definition at line 1593 of file log/log.c.


1593                                               {
1594   // Guard against recursion
1595   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1596     return;
1597   }
1598 
1599   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1600   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1601   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1602   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
1603     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = true;
1604     g_log.terminal_caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1605     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1606     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1607 
1608     // Now log the capabilities AFTER colors are set, so this log uses the correct colors
1609     log_debug("Terminal capabilities: color_level=%d, capabilities=0x%x, utf8=%s, fps=%d",
1610               g_log.terminal_caps.color_level, g_log.terminal_caps.capabilities,
1611               g_log.terminal_caps.utf8_support ? "yes" : "no", g_log.terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1612 
1613     // Now that we've detected once with reliable results, keep these colors consistent for all future logs
1614   }
1615   // Once initialized with reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1616 }

Force all terminal log output to stderr.

Set a custom log format string.


Parameters


format_str Format string with specifiers like

742                                                                             {

744   if (g_log.format) {
745     log_template_free(g_log.format);
746     g_log.format = NULL;
747   }
748   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
749     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
750     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
751   }
752 
753   /* Use default format if NULL or empty string */
754   const char *format_to_use = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0') ? format_str : OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT;
755   bool is_custom = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0');
756 
757   /* Parse the format string (always parse, never skip) */
758   log_template_t *parsed_format = log_template_parse(format_to_use, false);
759 
760   if (!parsed_format) {
761     log_error("Failed to parse log format: %s", format_to_use);
762     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid log format string");
763   }
764 
765   /* If console_only is true, we also need the default format for file output */
766   if (console_only && is_custom) {
767     log_template_t *default_format = log_template_parse(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT, false);
768 
769     if (!default_format) {
770       log_template_free(parsed_format);
771       log_error("Failed to parse default log format");
772       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to parse default format");
773     }
774     g_log.format = default_format;
775     g_log.format_console_only = parsed_format;
776   } else {
777     g_log.format = parsed_format;
778     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
779   }
780 
781   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.has_custom_format, is_custom);
782   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
783 }

698                                            {
699   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
700   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
701 
702   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
703   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
704   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
705   if (enabled) {
706     const options_t *opts = options_get();
707     if (opts && opts->quiet) {
708       return; // Silently ignore attempts to enable terminal output when --quiet is set
709     }
710   }
711 
712   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, enabled);
713 }

void
  log_shutdown_begin (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Begin shutdown phase - disable console logging but keep file
    logging. Call this before logging shutdown messages. Disables console output
    but keeps file logging so messages are recorded for debugging.




Useful when you want final messages (like "no servers
    found") to go to the log file only, not to stdout where it might
    interfere with output.

1783   if (g_log.shutdown_in_progress) {
1784     return; /* Already in shutdown phase */
1785   }
1786

1788   g_log.shutdown_saved_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
1789   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, false);
1790   g_log.shutdown_in_progress = true;
1791 }

void

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>

void
  log_system_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Shutdown the logging system internal state (system-level cleanup).
    Restores terminal state and clears registered session log buffer. Must be
    called once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).




Clears registered session log buffer. Color cleanup is handled
    separately in asciichat_shared_destroy() after memory reporting. Must
    be called once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).
